reason: Check implementation against task requirements, acceptance criteria, allowed scope, validation matrix, and Docker cold-start contract.
Verify Docker/infra contract, env/config alignment, validation matrix, Good/Base/Bad cases, and required checks.
Check schema/source-of-truth assumptions, Docker init-only-empty-volume semantics, and DB readiness diagnostics.
Review retrieval report, scope control, verification commands, and no duplicate backend path assumptions.
Review any new startup/readiness logs for level, usefulness, and secret safety.
Check retrieval-first decision and duplicate implementation risk.
Verify final healthchecks, depends_on conditions, env usage, and volume/init mounts.
Check backend healthcheck endpoint, timing, and any startup/wait changes.
Check web healthcheck/build/runtime changes if web startup behavior is adjusted.
Verify Docker profile remains container-local and does not depend on host/local config.
Check no unintended schema/business data changes and validate any chosen readiness table exists early and reliably.
Check cold-start docs, troubleshooting, and down -v destructive warning if updated.
Check English Docker docs if updated.
Check Chinese Docker docs if updated.
Check startup initialization remains narrow and does not hide schema/auth failures.
If health endpoint changes are considered, avoid breaking /api/site/meta semantics and repository contracts.
